mn42:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn42:10.1: And how, householders, are there three ways in which one behaves contrary to *dhamma* and improperly by mind?
mn42:10.2: Here, householders, someone is covetous … ‘Oh, if only what belongs to another were mine!’
mn42:10.3: And they have a mind of ill will [*byāpannacitto*], with corrupted intentions: ‘May these beings be killed … or may they not exist.’
mn42:10.4: And they have wrong view, distorted seeing:
mn42:10.5: ‘There is nothing given, nothing offered … no ascetics and brahmins who, having directly known and realized for themselves, declare this world and the next.’
mn42:10.6: In this way, householders, there are three ways in which one behaves contrary to *dhamma* and improperly by mind.
mn42:10.7: It is because of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and improper conduct, householders, that some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a plane of misery, a bad destination, the underworld, hell.
mn42:11.1: There are three ways, householders, in which one behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly by body, four ways in which one behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly by speech, and three ways in which one behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly by mind.
mn42:12.1: And how, householders, are there three ways in which one behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly by body?
mn42:12.2: Here, householders, someone, having abandoned the killing of living beings, refrains from killing living beings. With rod laid aside and sword laid aside, conscientious, compassionate, they dwell caring for the welfare of all living beings.
mn42:12.3: Having abandoned taking what is not given, they refrain from taking what is not given. Whatever belongings or property of another there are … they do not take what is not given, what is reckoned as stolen.
mn42:12.4: Having abandoned sexual misconduct … with such persons they do not engage in such conduct.
mn42:12.5: In this way, householders, there are three ways in which one behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly by body.
mn42:13.1: And how, householders, are there four ways in which one behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly by speech?
mn42:13.2: Here, householders, someone, having abandoned false speech, refrains from false speech. Whether in an assembly … they do not knowingly speak falsehood.
mn42:13.3: Having abandoned divisive speech … they speak speech that creates harmony.
mn42:13.4: Having abandoned harsh speech … they speak speech of such a kind.
mn42:13.5: Having abandoned idle chatter … they speak at the proper time, what is supported by reason, measured, and connected with benefit.
mn42:13.6: In this way, householders, there are four ways in which one behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly by speech.
mn42:14.1: And how, householders, are there three ways in which one behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly by mind?
mn42:14.2: Here, householders, someone is not covetous. Whatever belongings or property of another there are, they do not covet them: ‘Oh, if only what belongs to another were mine!’
mn42:14.3: And they have a mind free of ill will [*abyāpannacitto*], with uncorrupted intentions: ‘May these beings be free from enmity, free from affliction, free from trouble, and look after themselves happily.’
mn42:14.4: And they have right view, undistorted seeing:
mn42:14.5: ‘There is what is given, there is what is offered … there are ascetics and brahmins who, having directly known and realized for themselves, declare this world and the next.’
mn42:14.6: In this way, householders, there are three ways in which one behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly by mind.
mn42:14.7: It is because of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct, householders, that some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world.
mn42:15.1: If one who behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly should wish:
mn42:15.2: ‘Oh, if only with the breakup of the body, after death, I might be reborn in the company of great wealthy nobles,’
mn42:15.3: that would be possible: that with the breakup of the body, after death, that person would be reborn in the company of great wealthy nobles.
mn42:15.5: Because that person behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly.
mn42:16-17.1: If one who behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly should wish:
mn42:16-17.2: ‘Oh, if only with the breakup of the body, after death, I might be reborn in the company of great wealthy brahmins’ …
mn42:16-17.3: in the company of great wealthy householders,’
mn42:16-17.4: that would be possible: that with the breakup of the body, after death, that person would be reborn in the company of great wealthy householders.
mn42:16-17.6: Because that person behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly.
mn42:18-42.1: If one who behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly should wish:
mn42:18-42.11: the *devatās* who wield power over the creations of others …
mn42:18-42.13: that would be possible: that with the breakup of the body, after death, that person would be reborn in the company of the Brahmā-host *devatās*.
mn42:18-42.15: Because that person behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly.
mn42:18-42.16: If one who behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly should wish:
mn42:18-42.17: ‘Oh, if only with the breakup of the body, after death, I might be reborn in the company of the radiant *devatās*,’
mn42:18-42.18: that would be possible: that with the breakup of the body, after death, that person would be reborn in the company of the radiant *devatās*.
mn42:18-42.2: ‘Oh, if only with the breakup of the body, after death, I might be reborn in the company of the *devatās* of the Four Great Kings,’
mn42:18-42.20: Because that person behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly.
mn42:18-42.21: If one who behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly should wish:
mn42:18-42.22: ‘Oh, if only with the breakup of the body, after death, I might be reborn in the company of the limited-radiance *devatās* …
mn42:18-42.3: that would be possible: that with the breakup of the body, after death, that person would be reborn in the company of the *devatās* of the Four Great Kings.
mn42:18-42.34: the *devatās* who have reached the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite space …
mn42:18-42.35: the *devatās* who have reached the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇa*] …
mn42:18-42.36: the *devatās* who have reached the dimension [*āyatana*] of nothingness …
mn42:18-42.37: the *devatās* who have reached the dimension [*āyatana*] of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā*,’
mn42:18-42.38: that would be possible: that with the breakup of the body, after death, that person would be reborn in the company of the *devatās* who have reached the dimension [*āyatana*] of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā*.
mn42:18-42.40: Because that person behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly.
mn42:18-42.5: Because that person behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly.
mn42:18-42.6: If one who behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly should wish:
mn42:18-42.7: ‘Oh, if only with the breakup of the body, after death, I might be reborn in the company of the Thirty-three *devatās* …
mn42:2.1: Now at that time the brahmin householders of Verañjā were staying at Sāvatthī for some business.
mn42:2.2: The brahmin householders of Verañjā heard:
mn42:2.3: “The ascetic Gotama, sirs, the Sakyan son who went forth from a Sakyan clan, is dwelling at Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn42:2.4: And concerning Master Gotama this fine renown has arisen:
mn42:2.5: ‘Indeed, that *Bhagavā* is an *arahant*, a perfectly awakened one, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, gone to a good destination, knower of the world, unsurpassed trainer of persons to be tamed, teacher of *devatās* and humans, awakened, the *Bhagavā*.’
mn42:2.6: He declares this world with its *devatās*, its Māras, and its Brahmās, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, with its *devatās* and humans, having directly known and realized it for himself.
mn42:2.7: He teaches the *dhamma* that is good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased; he makes known a holy life that is entirely complete and pure.
mn42:2.8: It is good indeed to see such *arahants*.”
mn42:3.1: Then the brahmin householders of Verañjā went to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone to him, some paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side; some exchanged greetings with the *Bhagavā*, and after exchanging courteous and memorable talk, sat down to one side; some raised their joined palms toward the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side; some announced their name and clan in the presence of the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side; and some sat down to one side in silence. Seated to one side, the brahmin householders of Verañjā said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn42:4.1: “What, Master Gotama, is the cause, what is the condition, why some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a plane of misery, a bad destination, the underworld, hell?
mn42:4.2: And what, Master Gotama, is the cause, what is the condition, why some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world?”
mn42:43.1: If one who behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly should wish:
mn42:43.2: ‘Oh, if only with the destruction of the *āsavas* I might dwell, having directly known and realized for myself in this very life, the liberation of mind [*cetovimuttiṁ*] without *āsavas* and liberation by discernment [*paññāvimuttiṁ*], having entered upon it,’
mn42:43.3: that too would be possible: that with the destruction of the *āsavas* that person would dwell, having directly known and realized for himself in this very life, the liberation of mind [*cetovimuttiṁ*] without *āsavas* and liberation by discernment [*paññāvimuttiṁ*], having entered upon it.
mn42:43.5: Because that person behaves in accordance with *dhamma* and properly.’”
mn42:44.1: When this was said, the brahmin householders of Verañjā said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn42:44.3: Just as if one were to set upright what had been overturned, or uncover what was concealed, or point out the way to one who was lost, or hold up an oil lamp in the dark so that those with eyes might see forms, even so the *dhamma* has been made clear by Master Gotama in many ways.
mn42:44.4: We go for refuge to Master Gotama, and to the *dhamma*, and to the *saṅgha* of *bhikkhus* [*bhikkhusaṅghaṁ*].
mn42:44.5: May Master Gotama remember us as lay followers who from today have gone for refuge for life.”
mn42:44.6: The Verañjakasutta is finished, the second.
mn42:5.1: “Because of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and improper conduct, householders, some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a plane of misery, a bad destination, the underworld, hell.
mn42:5.2: Because of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct, householders, some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world.”
mn42:6.1: “We do not understand in detail the meaning of what Master Gotama has spoken in brief, without analyzing the meaning in detail.
mn42:6.2: It would be good if Master Gotama would teach the *dhamma* to us in such a way that we might understand in detail the meaning of what Master Gotama has spoken in brief, without analyzing the meaning in detail.”
mn42:6.3: “Then, householders, listen and attend carefully. I will speak.”
mn42:6.4: “Yes, sir,” the brahmin householders of Verañjā replied to the *Bhagavā*.
mn42:7.1: “There are three ways, householders, in which one behaves contrary to *dhamma* and improperly by body, four ways in which one behaves contrary to *dhamma* and improperly by speech, and three ways in which one behaves contrary to *dhamma* and improperly by mind.
mn42:8.1: And how, householders, are there three ways in which one behaves contrary to *dhamma* and improperly by body?
mn42:8.2: Here, householders, someone kills living beings. They are violent, blood-handed, given to beating and killing, without compassion for living creatures.
mn42:8.3: And they take what is not given. Whatever belongings or property of another there are … they take what is not given, what is reckoned as stolen.
mn42:8.4: And they engage in sexual misconduct. With those protected by mother … they engage in such conduct.
mn42:8.5: In this way, householders, there are three ways in which one behaves contrary to *dhamma* and improperly by body.
mn42:9.1: And how, householders, are there four ways in which one behaves contrary to *dhamma* and improperly by speech?
mn42:9.2: Here, householders, someone speaks falsehood. Whether in an assembly … they knowingly speak falsehood.
mn42:9.3: And they use divisive speech. Having heard here, they tell it elsewhere … they speak speech that creates division.
mn42:9.4: And they use harsh speech. They speak speech that is abrasive and rough … speech of such a kind.
mn42:9.5: And they engage in idle chatter. They speak at the wrong time … without limit and not connected with benefit.
mn42:9.6: In this way, householders, there are four ways in which one behaves contrary to *dhamma* and improperly by speech.
